design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally-scrolling levels while dodging enemy fire, collecting power-ups, and destroying waves of demons and bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing screen real estate to avoid dense enemy projectile patterns while maintaining offensive output
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as the angel Wor defending the land of Iccus against demonic invasion, wielding escalating magical firepower
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early 90s Genesis shmup with emphasis on boss design spectacle and visual clarity; praised for power-up variety and boss graphics, suggesting design prioritized weapon customization and enemy memorization over pure execution difficulty

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-up collection and weapon selection provide player agency in managing difficulty

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up based weapon and ability enhancement system
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through sequential horizontally-scrolling levels toward confrontation with the Destroyer
replayability_hooks: Different power-up paths and weapon configurations
Survival-based challenge in high-density enemy encounters

world_building_techniques: Simple high-concept premise: celestial conflict between heaven and demonic forces
Named protagonist (Wor the angel) with clear objective
Thematic enemy design reflecting demonic invasion scenario

boss_design.philosophy: Visually distinctive and mechanically patterned encounters designed as spectacle; emphasized in marketing and reviews for graphical presentation

faction_indicators: Heavens (player faction) vs. Demonic legions (antagonist faction)
Angel protagonist framing creates celestial vs. infernal binary
danger_signals: Legions of demons and monsters as existential threat to Iccus
Named singular antagonist (the Destroyer) provides focused conflict
environmental_storytelling: Demon-infested landscape progression suggests active invasion scenario
Boss design serves as visual representation of escalating threat hierarchy
